sections.paragraph:
τρίβος, ου, ἡ (τρίβω ‘rub’; Hom. Hymns, Hdt. et al.; ins: APF 1, 1901, p. 221, 21; PRainer 42, 14; LXX; Jos., Ant. 15, 347) a defined track or route, path

ⓐ of a way that is familiar and well-worn a beaten (τρίβω) path Hm 6, 1, 3.

ⓑ path gener. Mt 3:3; Mk 1:3; Lk 3:4 (all three Is 40:3; cp. also SibOr 3, 777).

ⓒ fig. of established ways of conduct τὰς τρίβους τῆς ζωῆς (cp. Pr 16:17; SibOr 3, 721) Hs 5, 6, 3.—DELG s.v. τρίβω. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

PRainer PRainer = Papyrus Rainer (Archduke of Austria), s. CPR—Lists 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

gener. gener. = generally

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1015
